acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Positive fixture fails for forbidden import
- [ ] Negative fixture passes for allowed import
- [ ] Tests deterministic across environments
description:
**Problem**
Lint behavior can silently change and lose coverage.
design: Add targeted fixtures or tests in lint crate proving expected catches and allowlist behavior.
title: Add regression fixtures/tests for boundary lint behavior

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Limits struct covers every constant in REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19.
- [ ] Replication rejects oversize frames and batches at decode time.
- [ ] EventBody decoder rejects depth/map/array/byte-length violations.
- [ ] WAL append rejects records > MAX_WAL_RECORD_BYTES before write.
- [ ] Tests verify representative limits are enforced and surfaced as errors.
description:
**Problem**
The realtime plan hardcodes many safety limits (bytes, events, timeouts). Today these values are scattered or absent; different subsystems could silently diverge, which breaks the single canonical behavior the plan expects.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19 (normative defaults + safety bounds) and §9.8 (flow control)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (Limits struct and defaults)

**Files:** src/core/limits.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/daemon/repl/*, src/daemon/wal/*, src/git/checkpoint/import.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs
design:
**Design**
- Define a single Limits struct in src/core/limits.rs with all normative defaults (frame sizes, WAL bounds, CBOR decode bounds, HLC drift, backpressure, checkpoint limits).
- Introduce small unit newtypes (Bytes, Events, Millis) or clearly name fields to avoid unit confusion across modules.
- Thread Limits through EventBody decode, WAL append, replication framing, gap buffers, and IPC inflight control.
- Make all limit enforcement explicit at module boundaries; failures return ErrorPayload(code="limit_exceeded") with details of which limit tripped.
- Allow overrides via config, but enforce that defaults are the plan values when no config is provided.

notes.author: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
notes.content: Implemented Limits struct + defaults, config plumbing, IPC frame limit, WAL record size guard, and note/label limits with tests. Remaining for later phases: EventBody CBOR decode limits and replication frame/batch enforcement once those modules land.

title: Cross-phase: Normative defaults constants + enforcement

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A canonical command exists to profile fast and slow test tiers
- [ ] Baseline output identifies the slowest binaries/tests and the slowest setup phases
- [ ] Test-only instrumentation captures fixture/daemon/readiness timing without changing production behavior
- [ ] Results are easy to compare before/after later beads
closed_reason: The repo now has a stable profiling harness, shared fixture timing, and current before/after artifacts for both fast and slow tiers.
description:
**Problem**
We have stale profiling artifacts in-repo, but no current, repeatable baseline for which test binaries, individual tests, or setup phases dominate wall time on today's harness. That makes optimization work too easy to misdirect and too hard to verify.

**Design**
Add a reproducible profiling path built around `cargo nextest` timings plus targeted Instruments/Samply traces for the slowest test binaries. Add test-only timing instrumentation around fixture setup, daemon boot, replication readiness, and command round-trips so before/after comparisons tell us where wins actually came from.

**Files**
- `.cargo/config.toml`
- `.config/nextest.toml`
- `scripts/` (new or existing profiling scripts)
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/*.rs`
- `docs/PERF_HOTPATHS.md` or a focused test-perf doc

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Added repo-local `cargo xtest` alias, `.config/nextest.toml`, `scripts/profile-tests.sh`, and env-gated timing hooks in shared integration fixtures (`git`, `realtime`, `tailnet_proxy`, `repl_rig`).

Validation so far:
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration fixtures::timing::tests`
- `cargo nextest list --profile fast --workspace --all-features`
- `cargo nextest list --profile slow --workspace --all-features --features slow-tests`
- fast profiling artifact: `tmp/perf/tests-20260314-104053`

Current baseline receipts from that artifact:
- `beads-rs::integration` dominates fast-tier wall time at ~221.5s
- top instrumented setup phase is `fixture.repl_rig.new` at ~24.4s total
- new blocker bead filed: `beads-rs-fblt.6` for migration divergence failures surfaced by the fast baseline

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
The profiling/instrumentation path is now the canonical way to measure this epic.

Landed:
- `scripts/profile-tests.sh` for fast + slow nextest timing capture
- env-gated fixture timing in shared integration helpers (`timing.rs`, `wait.rs`, `bd_runtime.rs`, `repl_rig.rs`, `tailnet_proxy.rs`, `legacy_store.rs`, and related adopters)
- updated receipts/docs in `docs/PERF_HOTPATHS.md` and `AGENTS.md`

Current artifacts:
- failing baseline artifact: `tmp/perf/tests-20260314-104053`
- current passing profiling artifact: `tmp/perf/tests-20260314-122418` (`fast_exit_code=0`, `slow_exit_code=0`, `PROFILE_TEST_THREADS=2`)
- burn-in artifact: `tmp/perf/burnin-20260314-121518`

Current runner receipts:
- `cargo xtest --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow` -> `42.182s`
- `cargo nextest run --profile slow --workspace --all-features --features slow-tests --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow` -> `42.532s`

Verification on current stack:
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `just dylint`
- `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo xtest --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow`
- `cargo nextest run --profile slow --workspace --all-features --features slow-tests --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow`

title: Instrument fast and slow test tiers with reproducible timing baselines
